title: Anthropology of Islam in South Asia
further_info: Recommended Reading Abu-Lughod, Lila 2013: Do Muslim Women Need Saving? Harvard: Harvard University Press. 27–53. Ahmed, Asad Ali 2009: “Specters of Macaulay: Blasphemy, the Indian Penal Code, and Pakistan’s Postcolonial Predicament.” In: Kaur, Raminder and William Mazzarella (eds): Censorship in South Asia – Cultural Regulation from Sedition to Seduction. Bloomington and Indianapolis: Indiana University Press. 172–205. Baumann, Gerd 2010: “Islam.” In: Barnard, Alan and Jonathan Spencer (eds): The Routledge Encyclopedia of Social and Cultural Anthropology. Second Edition. London/ New York: Routledge. 386–387. Dandekar, Deepra 2024: “A Material Religion Approach to the Dargah of Sadal Baba in Pune” Nidān: International Journal for Indian Studies 9, 2. 17–34. Jaffer, Amen 2017: “Spiritualizing Marginality: Sufi Concepts and the Politics of Identity in Pakistan. Society and Culture in South Asia 3 (2). 175–197. Khan, Arsalan 2018: “Islam and Pious Sociality – The Ethics of Hierarchy in the Tablighi Jamaat in Pakistan”. In: Hickel, Jason and Naomi Haynes (eds): Hierarchy and Value – Comparative Perspectives on Moral Order. 97–114. Kreinath, Jens 2014 (ed.): The Anthropology of Islam Reader. Milton Park, et al.: Routledge. Vicini, Fabio 2024. “God is everywhere. Islam, Christianity, and the immanence of transcendence.” HAU: Journal of Ethnographic Theory 14 (1): 19–32. Vicini, Fabio, and Lilli Di Puppo 2024. “Rethinking the anthropological enterprise in light of Muslim ontologies. Secular vestiges, spiritual epistemologies, vertical knowledge”. HAU: Journal of Ethnographic Theory 14 (1): 7–18.
